rulings.Case Title: TITAN SERVICES INC., AN ARIZONA CORPORATION VS. PHOENIX ELECTRIC COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 16, 2011, line 4, PLAINTIFF TITAN SERVICES INC., AN ARIZONA CORPORATION MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE APPLIES TO THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. A.F. BROWN ELEC. CONTRACTOR, INC. V. RHINO ELEC. SUPPLY, INC., (2006) 137 CAL.APP.4TH 1118, 1128. CCP 425.16 APPLIES TO A SUIT COMPLAINING ABOUT A STOP NOTICE WHERE SUCH A FILING IS DONE WHEN A LAWSUIT IS CONTEMPLATED IN GOOD FAITH AND GIVEN SERIOUS CONSIDERATION. TITAN'S SUPPORTING DECLARATIONS MEET THAT TEST. SECOND, PARAGRAPH 38 OF THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION COMPLAINS THAT TITAN BREACHED THE SUBCONTRACT BY FILING A CAUSE OF ACTION TO RECOVER ON THE SURETY BOND. THAT COMPONENT CLEARLY IMPLICATES CCP 425.16. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARGUMENT THAT THE CLAIMS ARISE FROM THE SAME TRANSACTION IS TENUOUS. THE UNDERLYING CLAIM PERTAINS TO THE ELECTRICAL SERVICES AND WHETHER PAYMENT SHOULD BE MADE. THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION RELATES TO CROSS-DEFENDANT'S PROSECUTION OF A CLAIM IN COURT. FURTHERMORE, CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE HOW IT WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS BECAUSE IT HAS NOT SHOWN HOW FILING A STOP NOTICE AND FILING A CAUSE OF ACTION TO RECOVER ON A BOND VIOLATES THE SUBCONTRACT. FINALLY THE COURT AWARDS ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,541.50 TO PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT. =(302/LMG)
